title: Crisp Syrian Flatbread Grill
description: Sweet, crisp flatbread dish originating from Syria, perfect for a quick weeknight dinner. Made with yeast and a dairy-free dough, it's a delicious and satisfying meal.
instructions: Combine flour, water, and yeast in a large mixing bowl. Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with a damp cloth, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat. Punch down the dough and divide it into 4-6 equal pieces.
Roll out each piece into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
Brush both sides of each flatbread with olive oil and season with salt.
Place the flatbreads on the grill and cook for 1-2 minutes on each side, until they're crispy and golden brown.
Serve the flatbreads warm, garnished with fresh herbs or a squeeze of lemon juice.
Repeat with the remaining dough pieces, cooking in batches if necessary.
